title: Acceptance
description: An ordered array of typed, machine-checkable acceptance items for one devflow task. Each item is self-contained: its 'type' selects exactly one shape (oneOf), and every shape carries the fields a machine needs to PROVE the item without human judgement. A verifier replaying these items must be able to pass/fail each one objectively.

items.description: One machine-checkable acceptance item. The 'type' discriminator picks exactly one of the self-contained variants below.

oneOf.title: command
oneOf.description: Run a shell command; the item passes iff the process exit code matches expectation. Use for build/lint/format/typecheck/any binary gate.

cmd.description: Exact shell command to execute, runnable as-is from the repo root.

must_pass.description: true = command must exit 0; false = command must exit non-zero (a negative assertion).

cwd.description: Optional working directory (repo-relative) to run the command in; defaults to repo root.

timeout_s.description: Optional hard timeout in seconds; exceeding it fails the item.

expect_stdout.description: Optional substring/regex that stdout must contain for the item to pass.

oneOf.title: unit_test
oneOf.description: Run a unit-test selector and require all selected tests to pass. Use for fast, isolated, no-I/O tests.

cmd.description: Command that runs the targeted unit tests (e.g. 'go test ./pkg/foo/...', 'pytest tests/unit/test_x.py').

selector.description: Optional test name / pattern the suite must include (asserts the test actually exists and ran, not silently skipped).

must_pass.description: Whether the suite must exit green (default true).

oneOf.title: integration_test
oneOf.description: Run an integration/e2e-ish test that crosses a real boundary (DB, network, multiple modules). Use when isolation is impossible or the boundary is the point.

cmd.description: Command that runs the integration test(s).

selector.description: Optional test name / pattern that must be present in the run.

requires.description: External dependencies the test needs up (e.g. 'postgres', 'redis', 'staging-api'); a verifier provisions/checks these before running.

must_pass.description: Whether the suite must exit green (default true).

oneOf.title: runtime_probe
oneOf.description: Drive the RUNNING system over a route/endpoint and assert observable response facts. Use for HTTP routes, RPC methods, or UI flows verifiable against a live process.

method.description: Optional HTTP method; defaults to GET when omitted.

assertions.description: Observable, machine-checkable assertions about the live response. ALL must hold for the item to pass.

check.description: What facet of the response to assert.

path.description: JSONPath/header-name selector when check is json_path or header.

equals.description: Expected exact value (status code, header value, json value).

contains.description: Substring the targeted text must contain (body_contains / header).

oneOf.title: diff_assertion
oneOf.description: Constrain WHICH files the change is allowed to touch — enforces path ownership and prevents silent scope creep. A verifier diffs the branch against base and checks the touched set.

must_touch.description: Glob(s)/path(s) that the diff MUST modify (the work is incomplete if untouched).

must_not_touch.description: Glob(s)/path(s) that the diff MUST NOT modify (out-of-lane edits fail the item).

base.description: Optional base ref to diff against; defaults to the epic branch's merge-base.

oneOf.title: contract_assertion
oneOf.description: Assert that an interface/contract artifact (OpenAPI, protobuf, JSON Schema, GraphQL SDL, exported type surface) conforms or did not break compatibility. Use for API/schema stability gates.

artifact.description: Repo-relative path to the contract artifact (e.g. 'api/openapi.yaml', 'proto/order.proto').

against.description: Optional reference the artifact is compared/validated against (e.g. a baseline ref, a schema file).

cmd.description: Optional command that performs the contract check (e.g. 'buf breaking', 'oasdiff'); its exit code decides the item.

oneOf.title: migration_dry_run
oneOf.description: Apply a schema/data migration in dry-run (non-destructive) mode and require it to succeed and be reversible. Use to gate any migration WITHOUT touching prod data (the destructive path is a tripwire, never automated).

cmd.description: Dry-run/plan command that exercises the migration without committing (e.g. 'migrate up --dry-run', 'alembic upgrade head --sql').

rollback_cmd.description: Command proving the migration is reversible (down/rollback in dry-run); recommended for any forward migration.

must_be_reversible.description: Whether a working rollback path is required for the item to pass (default true).

oneOf.title: performance_budget
oneOf.description: Assert a measured metric stays within a budget. Use to gate latency/throughput/size/allocation regressions. The metric MUST be measured against the live/built artifact, never guessed.

max.description: Upper bound the measured metric must not exceed.

min.description: Optional lower bound (for metrics where higher is the regression, e.g. throughput/score).

unit.description: Unit of the metric (ms, kb, ops/s, score) for unambiguous reporting.

measure_cmd.description: Command that produces the measurement (e.g. a benchmark/lighthouse run); its output supplies the value compared to max/min.
